title: rubus villicaulis subsp. insularis
intro: Rubus villicaulis subsp. insularis, also known as the Hawaiian raspberry or insular raspberry, is a rare and endangered species of raspberry native to Hawaii. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds that have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Rubus villicaulis subsp. insularis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antitumor properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, although its use is largely limited due to its rarity and conservation status.
